0.original: It holds true not only on the golf course, but in every facet of life.
0.b1: This is true not only in golf, but in every part of life.
0.cn: 这不仅适用于高尔夫球场，也适用于生活的方方面面。
0.ai_notes:
'holds true': remains valid or applicable.
'facet of life': a particular aspect or side of life.
0.remarks:
'holds true': 依然有效或适用。
'facet of life': 生活的一个方面或侧面。

1.original: Four things you can do to lick health excusitis.
1.b1: Four things you can do to stop making excuses about your health.
1.cn: 你可以做四件事来克服对健康问题的借口。
1.ai_notes:
'lick': to overcome or defeat a problem.
'excusitis': a humorous, made-up word combining 'excuse' and '-itis' (a suffix for inflammation/disease) to describe the habit of making excuses.
1.remarks:
'lick': 克服或战胜困难。
'excusitis': 一个幽默的自造词，结合了“借口”和“-itis”（炎症/疾病后缀），用来描述找借口的习惯。

2.original: The best vaccine against health excusitis consists of these four doses.
2.b1: The best way to prevent health excuses is to follow these four steps.
2.cn: 预防健康借口的最好方法就是遵循这四点建议。
2.ai_notes: 'vaccine': a substance used to prevent disease; here used metaphorically as a way to prevent a bad habit.
2.remarks: 'vaccine': 疫苗；此处隐喻为预防坏习惯的方法。

4.original: Refuse to talk about your health.
4.b1: Stop talking about your health problems.
4.cn: 拒绝谈论你的健康状况。
4.ai_notes: 'Refuse to': to decide firmly not to do something.
4.remarks: 'Refuse to': 坚定地决定不做某事。

5.original: The more you talk about an ailment, even the common cold, the worse it seems to get.
5.b1: The more you talk about an illness, even a cold, the worse it feels.
5.cn: 你越是谈论疾病，哪怕只是普通感冒，感觉就会越糟糕。
5.ai_notes: 'ailment': a minor illness or health problem.

6.original: Talking about bad health is like putting fertilizer on weeds.
6.b1: Talking about poor health is like giving fertilizer to weeds.
6.cn: 谈论糟糕的健康状况就像给杂草施肥一样。
6.ai_notes: 'fertilizer on weeds': a metaphor meaning that giving attention to a negative thing (like an illness) makes it grow stronger.
6.remarks: 'fertilizer on weeds': 隐喻，意为关注负面事物（如疾病）会使其变本加厉。

7.original: Besides, talking about your health is a bad habit.
7.b1: Also, talking about your health is a bad habit.
7.cn: 此外，谈论你的健康状况是一个坏习惯。
7.ai_notes: 'Besides': used to add another reason or point.
7.remarks: 'Besides': 用于补充另一个理由或观点。

8.ai_notes: 'bores': makes someone feel uninterested or tired.

9.original: It makes one appear self-centered and old maidish.
9.b1: It makes you seem self-centered and old-fashioned.
9.cn: 这会让人觉得你以自我为中心，而且老气横秋。
9.ai_notes:
'self-centered': thinking only about oneself.
'old maidish': an old-fashioned, slightly insulting term suggesting someone is fussy, boring, or overly concerned with minor details.
9.remarks:
'self-centered': 只顾自己，以自我为中心。
'old maidish': 一个过时且略带贬义的词，形容人挑剔、无聊或过于纠结琐事。

10.original: Success-minded people defeat the natural tendency to talk about their bad health.
10.b1: Successful people avoid the natural urge to talk about their health problems.
10.cn: 追求成功的人会克服那种谈论自己健康问题的本能倾向。
10.ai_notes: 'Success-minded': people who are focused on achieving their goals.

11.original: One may, and let me emphasize the word may, get a little sympathy,
11.b1: You might, and I mean might, get a little sympathy,
11.cn: 你或许——请注意是或许——能得到一点同情，
11.ai_notes: 'emphasize': to give special importance to a word or idea.

12.original: but one doesn't get respect and loyalty by being a chronic complainer.
12.b1: but you will not get respect if you always complain.
12.cn: 但如果你总是抱怨，是不会赢得尊重和忠诚的。
12.ai_notes: 'chronic complainer': someone who complains all the time as a regular habit.

14.original: Refuse to worry about your health.
14.ai_notes: 'Refuse to worry': to make a conscious choice not to be anxious.
14.remarks: 'Refuse to worry': 有意识地选择不焦虑。

15.original: Dr. Walter Alvarez, emeritus consultant to the world-famous Mayo Clinic, wrote recently,
15.b1: Dr. Walter Alvarez, a famous expert at the Mayo Clinic, recently wrote:
15.cn: 梅奥诊所的著名退休顾问沃尔特·阿尔瓦雷斯医生最近写道：
15.ai_notes:
'emeritus': a title for a retired professional who keeps their honorary rank.
'Mayo Clinic': a world-famous medical center in the United States known for high-quality care.
15.remarks:
'emeritus': 退休后保留荣誉职衔的专业人员头衔。
'Mayo Clinic': 美国著名的医疗中心，以高质量护理闻名。

16.original: I always beg worriers to exercise some self-control.
16.b1: I always tell people who worry to control themselves.
16.cn: 我总是恳求那些爱担忧的人要学会自我克制。
16.ai_notes:
'beg': to ask very strongly or earnestly.
'self-control': the ability to manage one's own emotions and actions.
16.remarks:
'beg': 恳求或强烈要求。
'self-control': 控制自己情绪和行为的能力。

17.original: For instance, when I saw this man, a fellow who was convinced he had a diseased gallbladder,
17.b1: For example, I once saw a man who was sure he had a sick gallbladder,
17.cn: 例如，我曾见过一个人，他坚信自己的胆囊有病，
17.ai_notes:
'fellow': a casual way to refer to a man.
'gallbladder': a small organ that helps with digestion.
17.remarks:
'fellow': 对男性的非正式称呼。
'gallbladder': 胆囊，一种辅助消化的器官。

18.original: although eight separate x-ray examinations showed that the organ was perfectly normal,
18.b1: even though eight X-rays showed his gallbladder was perfectly healthy.
18.cn: 尽管八次不同的X光检查都显示他的器官完全正常，
18.ai_notes: 'x-ray examinations': medical tests that use radiation to look inside the body.
18.remarks: 'x-ray examinations': 使用辐射检查身体内部的医学检查。

19.original: I begged him to quit getting his gallbladder x-rayed.
19.b1: I begged him to stop getting more X-rays.
19.cn: 我还是恳求他不要再去做胆囊X光检查了。

20.original: I have begged hundreds of heart-conscious men to quit getting electrocardiograms made.
20.b1: I have asked many people who worry about their hearts to stop getting constant heart tests.
20.cn: 我已经恳求过数百名担心心脏的人停止频繁进行心电图检查。
20.ai_notes:
'heart-conscious': people who are overly worried about their heart health.
'electrocardiograms': medical tests that record the electrical activity of the heart.
20.remarks:
'heart-conscious': 过度担心心脏健康的人。
'electrocardiograms': 记录心脏电活动的医学检查（心电图）。

22.original: Be genuinely grateful that your health is as good as it is.
22.b1: Be truly thankful that your health is as good as it is.
22.cn: 真心感谢你现在拥有的健康。

23.original: There's an old saying worth repeating often.
23.b1: There is an old saying that is worth remembering.
23.cn: 有一句老话值得经常重温。
23.ai_notes: 'worth repeating': important enough to be said again.
23.remarks: 'worth repeating': 重要到值得再说一遍。

24.original: I felt sorry for myself because I had ragged shoes until I met a man who had no feet.
24.b1: I felt sad about my old shoes until I met a man who had no feet.
24.cn: 我曾因为自己穿着破旧的鞋子而感到难过，直到我遇见了一个没有双脚的人。
24.ai_notes: 'ragged': old, torn, or in poor condition.

25.original: Instead of complaining about not feeling good, it's far better to be glad you are as healthy as you are.
25.b1: Instead of complaining, it is better to be happy that you are healthy.
25.cn: 与其抱怨身体不适，不如为自己目前的健康状况感到庆幸。

26.original: Just being grateful for the health you have is powerful vaccination against developing new aches and pains and real illness.
26.b1: Being grateful for your health is a great way to prevent new illnesses.
26.cn: 仅仅是对你拥有的健康心存感激，就是预防新病痛和真正疾病的强效疫苗。
26.ai_notes: 'vaccination': a protective measure; here it means a strong defense against negative thoughts.
26.remarks: 'vaccination': 预防措施；此处指抵御负面思想的强力防御。

28.original: Remind yourself often, it's better to wear out than rust out.
28.b1: Remember: it is better to be active and wear out than to be inactive and rust out.
28.cn: 经常提醒自己，与其闲置生锈，不如忙碌磨损。
28.ai_notes:
'wear out': to become tired or damaged through hard work or activity.
'rust out': to become useless or weak because of inactivity.
28.remarks:
'wear out': 因过度工作或活动而疲惫或损坏。
'rust out': 因缺乏活动而变得无用或虚弱。

29.cn: 生活是属于你去享受的。
29.ai_notes: 'Life is yours to enjoy': a common phrase meaning you have the responsibility and right to be happy.
29.remarks: 'Life is yours to enjoy': 一句常用语，意为你有责任也有权利去享受生活。

30.ai_notes: 'waste it': to spend time or life without purpose.
30.remarks: 'waste it': 虚度光阴或毫无目的地浪费生命。

31.original: Don't pass up living by thinking yourself into a hospital bed.
31.b1: Do not stop living just because you worry yourself into a hospital bed.
31.cn: 不要因为总是担心而把自己想进医院，从而放弃了生活。
31.ai_notes:
'pass up': to miss an opportunity or ignore something.
'thinking yourself into a hospital bed': a figurative way of saying that constant worry can actually make you physically sick.
31.remarks:
'pass up': 错过机会或忽视某事。
'thinking yourself into a hospital bed': 一种比喻说法，意为持续的担忧真的会让你生病住院。
